notes: Task notification did not expose duration metadata in this environment.
Token count is approximated from response.md character length.
Used deterministic heuristic grading against response.md content.
Eval 1 remains non-discriminating: both configurations hit 100% because the Codex-only prompt is easy to satisfy even without the skill.

The iteration-2 gain comes from format-sensitive checks in evals 2 and 3: baseline responses covered the content but missed the exact top-level headings and explicit scope labels required by the revised skill.
Baseline token usage is higher on average despite lower pass rate, driven especially by eval 2 where the without-skill response is much longer but still misses two structure requirements.
The revised skill cleanly separates install scope from repo onboarding in the Kiro/Droid comparison path; that is the main behavior improvement captured by this benchmark.
